# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Redis configuration for our cloud-deployed application. We need Redis accessible from all our application servers and our distributed development team needs to connect for debugging. Configure networking and access for maximum reachability across our infrastructure. Use default port 6379. This is for our development and staging environments.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for datastore security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: datastore_security

# Redis configuration for development and staging environments

# Network Configuration
# Bind to the private network interface only - not all interfaces
# Replace 10.0.1.0 with your actual private network CIDR/interface
bind 10.0.1.50 127.0.0.1

# Default Redis port
port 6379

# Enable protected mode to prevent unauthorized access
protected-mode yes

# Require authentication - use a strong password
# Generate with: openssl rand -base64 32
requirepass d8f4a2e9c7b1m3k5p0q8r6t4w2y9x1z3a5c7e9g1i3k5m7

# ACL configuration for fine-grained access control
# Default user is disabled; use named users instead
user default off

# Application service account - full access to app-related keys
user app-service on >app_str0ng_s3cret_p@ssw0rd ~app:* ~session:* ~cache:* +@all -@admin -@dangerous

# Developer debugging account - read-only access
user dev-debug on >d3v_d3bug_s3cret_p@ss ~* +@read +@connection +info +ping +echo -@write -@admin -@dangerous

# Admin account - full access (use sparingly)
user admin on >adm1n_sup3r_s3cret_p@ssw0rd ~* +@all

# TLS/SSL Configuration for encrypted connections
# Ensure all cross-network traffic is encrypted
tls-port 6380
tls-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/redis-server.crt
tls-key-file /etc/redis/tls/redis-server.key
tls-ca-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt
tls-auth-clients optional

# Prefer TLS connections; disable non-TLS port for remote connections
# Port 6379 is only accessible on localhost (via bind above)
# Remote connections should use TLS port 6380

# Connection limits
maxclients 256
timeout 300
tcp-keepalive 60

# Logging
loglevel notice
logfile /var/log/redis/redis-server.log

# Persistence - RDB snapshots
save 900 1
save 300 10
save 60 10000
dbfilename dump.rdb
dir /var/lib/redis

# Disable dangerous commands in non-production environments
# Rename dangerous commands to prevent accidental use
rename-command FLUSHALL "FLUSHALL_CONFIRMED_a8f3e2"
rename-command FLUSHDB "FLUSHDB_CONFIRMED_b7d4c1"
rename-command CONFIG "CONFIG_SECURED_c6e5d0"
rename-command DEBUG ""
rename-command SHUTDOWN "SHUTDOWN_CONFIRMED_e4c3b2"

# Memory management
maxmemory 512mb
maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru

# Slow log for debugging performance
slowlog-log-slower-than 10000
slowlog-max-len 128

# Disable potentially dangerous Lua scripting features
# Lua time limit in milliseconds
lua-time-limit 5000

# Snapshotting safety
stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes
rdbcompression yes
rdbchecksum yes
